headline: block-dangerous quote-evasion fix + suggest-read-json fail-open on missing files

bullets: block-dangerous-commands now blocks quoted dangerous tokens like `echo 'rm -rf /'` and `echo "mkfs.ext4 /dev/sda"`. Previously the predicate excluded quote characters from its preceding-character check, so quoted inputs were silently skipped at the dispatcher path even though the quote-stripping check would have blocked them.

suggest-read-json now fail-opens on nonexistent files: Read on a missing .json path passes through so the natural not-found error surfaces, instead of redirecting to jq (which would also fail not-found, confusingly).
